title: trifolium campestre var. minus
intro: Trifolium campestre var. minus, also known as the small field clover or small hop clover, is a plant species within the legume family that is characterized by its small white or pink flowers and trifoliate leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Trifolium campestre var. minus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
